heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks at Cradle Mountain
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Cradle Mountain? We compared the family holiday and caravan park options at Cradle Mountain so you can quickly find the right base for your kids, budget and travel style.
Cradle Mountain is Tasmania's alpine showpiece, and with kids it's about picking the right walks, packing for four seasons in a day and choosing a base that keeps the evenings interesting.
local_knowledge: Accommodation at Cradle is scarce and the camping option comes with a twist worth having: Cradle Mountain Fishery and Camping runs a trout fishery on-site, so kids can catch dinner and cook it, which reliably beats any playground. The Dove Lake circuit is the family walk, 6 kilometres of boardwalk and track that school-aged kids finish proudly; the Enchanted Walk near the visitor centre suits littler legs at 20 minutes. Access to Dove Lake is by shuttle bus from the visitor centre for most of the day, buy parks passes and shuttle tickets together. Devils@Cradle runs evening feeding tours where kids meet Tasmanian devils up close. Pack thermals in January, it can snow any month of the year.

if_we_were_booking.label: Catch your dinner
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Cradle Mountain Fishery and Camping, where the on-site trout fishery turns the afternoon into catch-and-cook and the campfire does the entertaining. It's the only true camping-style base at the mountain.

if_we_were_booking.label: Different style
if_we_were_booking.text: If we wanted cabins with more comfort in the cold, we'd look at the lodge-style options near the visitor centre and keep the fishery as a day visit, the fishing is open to visitors too.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Book the Devils@Cradle night feeding tour. Watching Tasmanian devils feed a few metres away is the story your kids tell when they get home, and evening is when Cradle needs a plan.

faq.question: Can kids walk the Dove Lake circuit?
faq.answer: Yes, it's the classic family walk. Six kilometres of mostly boardwalk taking two to three hours with kids, flat enough for school-aged children to finish proudly.

faq.question: What is the best short walk for little kids?
faq.answer: The Enchanted Walk near the visitor centre, about 20 minutes through mossy rainforest along a creek, with a real chance of wombats late in the day.

faq.question: How does the Dove Lake shuttle work?
faq.answer: Private vehicle access to Dove Lake is restricted through the day, so most visitors ride the shuttle from the visitor centre. Buy shuttle tickets with your parks pass.

faq.question: What is the camping like at Cradle Mountain?
faq.answer: Cradle Mountain Fishery and Camping offers a bush camping experience with an on-site trout fishery where kids can catch and cook their own dinner, which is the park's genuine drawcard.

faq.question: When can kids see Tasmanian devils?
faq.answer: Devils@Cradle sanctuary runs day visits and evening feeding tours year-round. The night feeding is the standout for families.

faq.question: What should you pack for kids?
faq.answer: Thermals, beanies and waterproof layers in every season. Cradle can serve sun, rain and snow in one afternoon, including in summer.

faq.question: Will kids see wombats?
faq.answer: Very likely. Ronny Creek at dusk is the most reliable wombat spotting in Tasmania, watch from the boardwalk and keep your distance.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit with kids?
faq.answer: December to March brings the mildest weather and longest days. Winter is spectacular with snow but demands proper cold-weather gear and flexible plans.

faq.question: Is there phone coverage and food up there?
faq.answer: Coverage is patchy to none past the visitor centre, and food options are limited. Stock up in Sheffield or Devonport and download maps before you arrive.

faq.question: How many nights do you need?
faq.answer: Two nights covers Dove Lake, the short walks and the devils. Three gives you weather insurance, which at Cradle is worth having.

hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park near Cradle Mountain means your family can wake up to fresh mountain air and spend days exploring nature trails before cozying up together by the fire each evening.
